evals.prompt: Parse these discovery notes from our call with Citizens Bank and generate the full output: the structured JSON profile, customer confirmation document, and internal gap report.
evals.expected_output: Three files: citizens-bank-discovery.json (valid JSON with all pain points, champion_enablement demo type, competitive context captured), citizens-bank-confirmation.md (customer-appropriate, uses verbatim quotes like 'scattered everywhere', no internal sales language), citizens-bank-gaps.md (budget and timeline flagged as low-impact gaps)
expectations: Produces a valid JSON file named citizens-bank-discovery.json
JSON correctly classifies demo_scope.recommended_type as champion_enablement
JSON captures at least 3 pain points including data fragmentation and automation bottlenecks
JSON identifies Michael De La Cruz as champion role, Joe Burke as decision_maker
JSON captures competitive context: Bain and AWS Bedrock as active alternatives
Confirmation doc does NOT mention Bain, AWS Bedrock, or any competitor
Confirmation doc uses customer's own language (e.g. 'scattered everywhere' or similar verbatim)
Confirmation doc does NOT use internal sales terms like 'champion', 'deal stage', or 'ICP'
Gap report identifies budget and timeline as missing fields
JSON deal_context.stage is champion_building or early_awareness

evals.prompt: I've got raw meeting notes from our IHG Club Vacations call — can you parse these and give me the structured discovery output?
evals.expected_output: Three files: ihg-club-discovery.json (technical specs extracted precisely: 3 servers, 50GB RAM, 220MM documents, 36GB index, 4 environments; multiple contacts with inferred roles; migration_assessment demo type), ihg-club-confirmation.md (shorter doc reflecting migration focus, gaps prominent), ihg-club-gaps.md
expectations: Produces a valid JSON file named ihg-club-discovery.json
JSON correctly classifies elastic_relationship.status as existing_on_prem
JSON correctly classifies elastic_relationship.fallen_behind_releases as true
JSON extracts infrastructure.server_count as 3 and infrastructure.ram_gb as 50
JSON extracts data_volumes with 220MM documents mentioned
JSON captures at least 4 customer contacts (Terry Nixon and others)
JSON correctly classifies demo_scope.recommended_type as migration_assessment
Typos in the notes are cleaned in structured output (not 'yeards', 'sice', 'dignostic')
Gap report exists and identifies gaps since no clear feature demo was scoped
JSON deal_context.stage is migration

evals.prompt: Structure the Thermo Fisher discovery notes and generate the confirmation doc we'll send to Karen before Thursday's demo.
evals.expected_output: Three files: thermo-fisher-discovery.json (urgent deal, RAG demo type, 27 upcoming projects, 30-40 years of historical data, partial_fragmented elastic relationship), thermo-fisher-confirmation.md (specific demo scenario about querying historical project data, tight timeline, Karen's language used), thermo-fisher-gaps.md
expectations: Produces a valid JSON file named thermo-fisher-discovery.json
JSON correctly classifies deal_context.urgency as high or critical
JSON correctly classifies elastic_relationship.status as partial_fragmented
JSON captures at least 3 pain points including institutional amnesia and manual overhead
JSON demo_scope.recommended_type includes rag_demo or technical_deep_dive
JSON captures Karen Pendergraft as decision_maker role
Confirmation doc includes a specific demo scenario referencing historical project data or similar concrete scope
Confirmation doc reflects the urgency (Q2 kickoff, vendor decision timing)
Confirmation doc does NOT use internal sales language
JSON next_steps.demo_timing references Thursday or the scheduled time

evals.prompt: Parse the Lowe's demo script as a discovery artifact and generate the discovery profile — this is our approved demo so treat the script as the post-discovery document.
evals.expected_output: Three files: lowes-discovery.json (retail industry, multi-persona audience, approved demo scoped, key features identified), lowes-confirmation.md (reflects what is already built/scoped, not what will be built), lowes-gaps.md
expectations: Produces a valid JSON file named lowes-discovery.json
JSON correctly identifies retail as the industry
JSON captures the three audience personas (Tom Gardner, Balajee Mohan, Pawan Kumar or similar)
JSON identifies key demo features: inventory intelligence, ML anomaly detection, ELSER/semantic search, agentic AI
JSON demo_scope.recommended_type reflects an approved technical demo (technical_deep_dive or similar)
Confirmation doc reflects that the demo is already scoped and built, not future-tense planning
JSON captures the Elastic team members (AJ Meyers as SA/presenter at minimum)
Gap report correctly notes this is an approved demo with few or no blocking gaps
